text: To set effective project goals, use the SMART framework. This means goals should be specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound. For example, instead of "improve the website," set a goal like "increase monthly site traffic by 20% within three months." Break the project into milestones, which are key dates marking progress, and define deliverables—tangible results like reports or prototypes. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved, including clients and team members. This prevents misunderstandings and keeps the project on track, as all parties agree on what success looks like. Clear goals turn vague ideas into actionable plans.
